tier_1.description: Single-document lookup — definition, simple rule, direct article reference

tier_2.description: Multi-condition reasoning — procedure with prerequisites, cross-article inference

note_on_judge: Judge LLM (Gemini) differs from generation LLM (Groq) to reduce self-evaluation bias.

naive_rag.description: ChromaDB vector similarity only — top-5, cosine distance, no rerank

hybrid_rag.description: Dense (MiniLM) + BM25 with Reciprocal Rank Fusion + cross-encoder rerank

agentic_rag.description: LangGraph intent router + hybrid retrieval + specialised tool calls per intent

corpus_gap.description: Question references a document not yet ingested into corpus
examples: q44: logistics licensing (Nghị định 163/2017 not in corpus)
q45: Luật Đất đai 2024 not ingested
q46: Luật Chứng khoán 2019 not in corpus
corpus_gap.fix: Expanded corpus to include 2 additional document types (commercial law, securities law) — reduced this failure category by 60%

retrieval_miss.description: Document exists in corpus but retriever returns wrong chunks
examples: q38: cross-doc synthesis retrieved single-document chunks instead of spanning both labor + tax law
q43: edge case question retrieved general corporate governance instead of specific article on contract authority
retrieval_miss.fix: Hybrid retrieval reduced this from 5 cases (naive) to 2 cases — remaining 2 require multi-hop retrieval not yet implemented

synthesis_error.description: Correct chunks retrieved but LLM synthesised incomplete or partially hallucinated answer
examples: q36: cross-doc labour + tax question — retrieved both relevant chunks but answer omitted the tax deductibility requirement for severance pay
synthesis_error.fix: Under investigation — likely requires explicit cross-doc synthesis prompt or structured extraction before generation

improvement_trajectory.observation: Each strategy reduces failure rate, but corpus_gap dominates remaining failures — retrieval quality is no longer the bottleneck

hybrid_vs_naive.verdict: BM25 + rerank substantially improves recall of exact legal terms (article numbers, law citations). Latency cost is 83% but acceptable for a legal Q&A use case where answer quality matters more than speed.

agentic_vs_hybrid.verdict: Agent routing adds modest quality lift for compare/summarize intents at cost of 75% latency. Main value is intent-specific handling, not retrieval quality. For simple lookup queries, hybrid alone is sufficient.

notes: Test set expanded from 20 to 50 questions across 3 tiers and 6 categories
Judge LLM changed from Groq to Gemini 1.5 Flash to eliminate self-evaluation bias
Tier 3 includes 3 out-of-corpus questions to measure hallucination guardrail
Corpus: 356 chunks from 18 Vietnamese legal documents
Environment: local CPU (Intel Core i7), no GPU
